headline: Stock market drops as geopolitical tensions push investors to sell
facts: India's Nifty50 index fell below 23,850 and the BSE Sensex dropped nearly 1,700 points on Friday. The decline was driven by US-Iran war tensions and rising crude oil prices affecting investor sentiment.
impact: Your mutual fund investments and stock portfolio likely took a hit today if you're holding Indian equities.
left_lens: Global instability and oil price shocks expose how vulnerable India's economy is to external geopolitical events beyond government control. This highlights the need for stronger domestic demand and reduced dependence on volatile global markets.
right_lens: International tensions in the Middle East directly threaten India's energy security and market stability. Strong foreign policy engagement and domestic economic resilience are critical to insulating markets from external shocks.
public_pulse: Young retail investors are anxious — trading apps saw panic selling as first-time stock market participants watched their portfolios bleed, with many questioning whether now is the time to stay invested or bail out.
original_title: Why did stock market crash today? Nifty50 ends below 23,850; Sensex down almost 1,700 points

headline: Lok Sabha approves extension for three bills in Rajya Sabha
facts: The Lok Sabha adopted a motion to extend the deadline for the Rajya Sabha's joint committee report on three bills: the Constitution 130th Amendment Bill 2025, the Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Bill 2025, and the Government of Union Territories Amendment Bill 2025. The Lok Sabha is also taking up matters of public importance during the Budget Session.
impact: The timeline for these three legislative pieces is now extended, meaning final parliamentary approval will take longer than originally scheduled.
left_lens: Opposition-aligned outlets would scrutinize whether the extension signals rushed legislation or inadequate deliberation on sensitive bills like J&K reorganisation, questioning if proper scrutiny is being bypassed.
right_lens: Government-aligned outlets would frame the extension as allowing thorough committee review and consensus-building across both houses before final passage of constitutional and administrative reforms.
public_pulse: Parliament observers and constitutional law enthusiasts are monitoring these bills closely; civil society groups focused on federalism and J&K autonomy are tracking the timeline and committee recommendations.
original_title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE: Rajya Sabha discusses Finance Bill, 2026

headline: AIADMK manifesto promises free refrigerators for ration card holders
facts: The AIADMK party released its manifesto for the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly election. Key promises include free refrigerators for women who hold PDS ration cards, revision of electricity tariffs for common areas in apartment buildings, and an increase in horizontal reservation for government school students in medical course admissions from 7.5% to 10.5%.
impact: Women from low-income households could receive free refrigerators if the party wins; apartment residents may see changes in electricity costs for shared spaces; more government school students could gain medical college seats.
left_lens: Welfare expansion shows commitment to economically weaker sections and addressing gender inequality. Increased medical reservation addresses educational equity by helping underprivileged government school students access professional courses.
right_lens: Targeted subsidies raise questions about fiscal sustainability and whether direct benefit transfers are the best use of state resources. Reservation increases may spark debate about merit-based admissions versus social equity priorities.
public_pulse: Women beneficiaries of public distribution systems view this as direct economic relief. Medical aspirants from government schools see expanded opportunities, while concerns exist about whether promises translate to delivery on the ground.
original_title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: AIADMK releases manifesto; assures free refrigerators to women ration card-holders

headline: Yograj Singh urges MS Dhoni to extend cricket career
facts: Yograj Singh has publicly praised MS Dhoni and suggested the Chennai Super Kings veteran should continue playing for another 10 years. The comment reflects admiration for Dhoni's performance and longevity in professional cricket.
impact: Cricket fans get reassurance that their favorite player might stick around longer than expected.
left_lens: Veteran cricketers like Dhoni represent a pathway for older players to maintain careers despite age bias in sports, challenging the industry's tendency to discard experienced talent for youth.
right_lens: Dhoni exemplifies individual excellence and durability — his ability to stay competitive at his age showcases the value of discipline, fitness, and mental toughness that builds national sporting pride.
public_pulse: CSK supporters are energized by this endorsement, hoping it signals Dhoni's commitment to staying in the game and leading their team for years to come.
original_title: ‘MS Dhoni should play for another 10 years’: Yograj Singh’s high praise for CSK veteran

headline: Anonymity in art depends on having the right identity
facts: The article examines how anonymity functions differently based on an artist's identity and privilege. It uses Banksy as a case study to show that remaining anonymous while building a global reputation requires certain advantages that not all artists possess equally.
impact: Artists from marginalized backgrounds may find it harder to protect their privacy while gaining recognition for their work.
left_lens: Systemic inequality means anonymity itself is a privilege — only those with existing social advantages can hide their identity while achieving fame, while artists from marginalized communities face greater scrutiny and exposure regardless of their wishes.
right_lens: Individual talent and merit eventually surface regardless of anonymity — if artwork is strong enough, the artist's identity becomes secondary to the quality of their work and their ability to market themselves.
public_pulse: Art students and emerging artists are frustrated by this disparity, noting on creative forums that their backgrounds make anonymity nearly impossible while doing the same work as more privileged counterparts.
original_title: Anonymity is a privilege. Banksy had to be a White man

headline: Cutting fuel duty costs government 7,000 crore rupees in two weeks
facts: The government cut excise duty on petrol and diesel by 10 rupees per litre. A top tax official says this move will cost the government 7,000 crore rupees in revenue over 15 days. The cut aims to reduce losses for oil companies during the Middle East conflict.
impact: Your fuel costs just dropped, but the government's tax collection took a hit to make it happen.
left_lens: Critics argue the government is prioritizing oil company profits over public funds that could go to schools and hospitals. They question why taxpayers must bear the burden when corporations face temporary losses from geopolitical events.
right_lens: Supporters say protecting oil companies from Middle East turmoil is crucial for energy security and economic stability. Lower fuel prices boost consumer spending and help businesses operate efficiently during uncertain times.
public_pulse: Commuters and daily wage workers are relieved by cheaper petrol and diesel, though some question how long the relief will last.
original_title: Excise duty cut on petrol, diesel could lead to  ₹7,000 crore revenue loss in 15 days: Top tax official

headline: Parliamentary panel warns only half of school education funds spent
facts: A Parliamentary panel found that only 51% of central school education funds were utilised. The panel flagged slow spending and delays in fund releases to certain states as key concerns.
impact: Students in some states may face delays in getting new school infrastructure, equipment, or programs if funds aren't released and spent faster.
left_lens: Government mismanagement and slow bureaucracy are failing students — the centre isn't pushing hard enough to ensure money reaches schools where it's needed most.
right_lens: State governments are dragging their feet on spending allocations — the centre is providing funds but implementation capacity at state level remains weak.
public_pulse: Teachers and parents in underfunded states are frustrated that allocated money sits unused while classrooms lack basic resources — this feels like funds exist but nobody's moving fast enough to use them.
original_title: Only 51% Of Central School Education Funds Utilised: Parliamentary Panel Flags Concern

headline: Government taxes diesel exports while cutting fuel duties for consumers
facts: The government imposed export duties on diesel and turbine fuel to ensure more domestic supply and strengthen energy security. It also reduced special excise duties on petrol and diesel to keep retail prices stable, while expecting to collect Rs 1,500 crore from the new export taxes.
impact: Your fuel bills stay steady at the pump, but India's oil industry faces higher costs when selling fuel abroad.
left_lens: This is a band-aid fix that shields consumers briefly while exporting inflation abroad. The real issue—India's dependence on global oil markets and weak refining capacity—remains unsolved. Export duties will only discourage investment in domestic fuel production over time.
right_lens: Smart policy balancing national interest with consumer welfare. Protecting domestic fuel supply during global instability matters more than short-term export revenue. This shows government prioritizing energy security and domestic stability over corporate profits.
public_pulse: Oil industry analysts and refinery operators are concerned—export restrictions could impact their margins and long-term investment plans during an already uncertain global energy situation.
original_title: Middle East crisis: Govt puts export duties on diesel, turbine oil; eyes Rs 1,500 cr collection

headline: Yograj Singh backs Dhoni to keep playing in IPL
facts: Former cricketer Yograj Singh has publicly supported MS Dhoni continuing his IPL career for another decade. Singh praised Dhoni's fitness, discipline, and dedication, arguing that performance should be the only measure for selection rather than age. He dismissed calls for Dhoni's retirement.
impact: Cricket fans debating Dhoni's future now have a senior player's endorsement that age shouldn't force retirement decisions in professional sports.
left_lens: Yograj's comments highlight how ageism in sports overlooks individual capability and fitness. The pressure to retire based on numbers rather than performance reflects systemic bias that could be challenged by merit-based selection alone.
right_lens: Yograj's backing emphasizes personal discipline and work ethic as the true measure of an athlete's worth. Dhoni's continued fitness demonstrates that dedication and traditional values of hard work can transcend age limits.
public_pulse: Cricket enthusiasts on social media are rallying behind Dhoni, using Yograj's praise as ammunition against retirement critics who they see as underestimating the champion's hunger and physical condition.
original_title: ‘Hats off to him’: Yograj’s rare praise for Dhoni, slams retirement calls

headline: Allahabad HC rules live-in relationships not criminal for married men
facts: The Allahabad High Court ruled that a married man living with a consenting adult woman does not commit a criminal offence. The Court stated that legal principles should remain separate from societal morality and individual views.
impact: Married individuals now have legal clarity that cohabitation with another consenting adult cannot be prosecuted as a crime under current law.
left_lens: Progressive commentators frame this as a win for individual liberty and personal autonomy. They argue the Court correctly prioritized constitutional rights over regressive social norms and conservative moral policing.
right_lens: Conservative critics argue the ruling undermines traditional family values and marriage sanctity. They contend courts should not dismiss societal morality when deciding cases affecting social institutions.
public_pulse: Family law experts and matrimonial rights advocates see this as a landmark judgment protecting personal choice. Social media shows concern from traditionalists who worry this weakens marriage as an institution.
original_title: No crime if married man lives with consenting adult woman, says Allahabad HC; insists on keeping law, morality apart

headline: PM Modi to meet Chief Ministers virtually on lockdown concerns
facts: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will hold a virtual meeting with Chief Ministers scheduled for 6:30 pm. The government has dismissed chances of a lockdown ahead of this meeting. The meeting is being held to discuss current concerns.
impact: Citizens waiting for clarity on government's economic and health policy direction should get answers from this high-level discussion.
public_pulse: Business owners and daily wage workers are watching closely — lockdown talk sparks panic about livelihoods, while others see the dismissal as reassuring news for the economy.
original_title: PM Modi Meeting LIVE Updates: Govt dismisses lockdown chances ahead of PM-CM virtual meet

headline: Indian farmers switching to dragon fruit for bigger profits
facts: Indian farmers are growing dragon fruit as an alternative crop to traditional options like mangoes and coffee. The shift is driven by the fruit's potential to generate higher income for farming families.
impact: Farmers in fruit-growing regions now have a new cash crop option that could improve their household earnings.
left_lens: Agricultural economists point out that crop diversification like this is necessary because traditional farming on small plots leaves families vulnerable to price crashes and climate shocks. Dragon fruit adoption shows how farmers are adapting to survive, but real support requires investment in irrigation, storage, and fair market access — not just leaving them to figure it out alone.
right_lens: This demonstrates Indian agricultural entrepreneurship at its best — farmers making smart business decisions to increase yields and income without waiting for government subsidies. Dragon fruit's profitability proves that when farmers have market freedom and access to new crops, they can compete globally and strengthen rural economies through their own initiative.
public_pulse: Small-scale farmers in mango and coffee-growing regions are cautiously exploring dragon fruit cultivation, seeing it as a hedge against unpredictable monsoons and volatile commodity prices that have squeezed margins for years.
original_title: The spiky cactus fruit giving Indian farmers a cash boost

headline: War threatens fertiliser supplies for India's next planting season
facts: The war is disrupting fertiliser shipments to India. Analysts say current stocks are enough for the upcoming sowing season, but they warn supplies could become tight if the conflict continues longer.
impact: Farmers might face fertiliser shortages later this year if the war doesn't end soon.
left_lens: Supply chain vulnerabilities expose India's dependence on imports during geopolitical crises. Government should have built strategic reserves and invested in domestic fertiliser production to protect vulnerable farmers from global shocks.
right_lens: India's fertiliser stockpiles demonstrate sound resource management and preparedness. The nation must strengthen supply chain resilience and reduce reliance on unstable regions to ensure agricultural security and national self-sufficiency.
public_pulse: Farmers are watching this closely — those dependent on imported fertilisers are already calculating costs and planning purchases before prices spike further.
original_title: Fertiliser supplies under strain as war disrupts shipments

headline: Apple opens Siri to rival AI chatbots in iOS update
facts: Apple is ending OpenAI's exclusive deal with Siri by introducing an "Extensions" feature in iOS 27 that lets users choose from multiple AI chatbots including Google Gemini and Anthropic Claude. Users will select their preferred AI from the App Store and configure it in Settings. Apple will earn a commission from chatbot subscriptions sold through its platform.
impact: iPhone users will get more choices for which AI powers their voice assistant instead of being locked into one option.
left_lens: Breaking up corporate monopolies benefits consumers and creates fair competition. Apple's move away from exclusivity signals that tech giants shouldn't lock users into single vendors, especially for essential services like voice assistants.
right_lens: Apple's diversification strategy strengthens its ecosystem and revenue streams. Allowing multiple AI options while taking a cut of subscriptions shows how businesses can grow by opening platforms while maintaining profitability and control.
public_pulse: Tech-savvy users and smartphone power users are likely pleased—they've long complained about being forced to use Siri when they prefer other AI assistants, and this finally gives them the freedom to switch.
original_title: Apple may end its 2-year-old exclusive deal with OpenAI that made Elon Musk angry

headline: RCB prepares ideal XI for IPL 2026 season opener against Sunrisers
facts: Indian Express has analyzed the playing XI that Royal Challengers Bangalore should field for their IPL 2026 season opener against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The article focuses on team composition strategy for this match.
impact: RCB fans will know which players to watch in the opening game and can debate whether the suggested XI matches the team's actual strategy.
public_pulse: IPL fantasy league players are closely studying these XI predictions to make informed squad picks before the season kicks off.
original_title: RCB IPL 2026: Ideal playing XI for Royal Challengers Bengluru vs Sunrisers Hyderabad in season opener
